mapping.a_element: fusion and braiding rules of the anyonic excitations (the modular-tensor-category data of the topological phase)
mapping.b_element: the weak Hopf algebra whose representation category can serve as the algebraic host for such fusion/braiding data

brief_justification: The only link is thematic: anyon fusion/braiding (paper A) is categorically describable by structures like weak Hopf algebras (paper B), but the actual machinery differs decisively. Paper A's method is an exact free-fermion reduction in a Z2 gauge field plus topological classification by a spectral Chern number; paper B's method is algebraic structure theory (Radford's antipode formula, antipode order, Tr(S^2) semisimplicity criteria). No role-for-role correspondence between a governing process, equation, or threshold holds, so they do not share an underlying method.
